name: DMZ Reverse Tunnel
notes: This rule will fire when connections seemed to be bridged across the network's DMZ through a reverse tunnel.
bb_references: BB:CategoryDefinition: Post DMZ Jump in order from the same source IP from the previous sequence
conditions_text: when at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Pre Reverse DMZ Jump, in order, with the same source IP followed by at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Post DMZ Jump in order from the same source IP from the previous sequence, within 1 minutes
thresholds: when at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Pre Reverse DMZ Jump, in order, with the same source IP followed by at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Post DMZ Jump in order from the same source IP from the previous sequence, within 1 minutes
TD_list: APPLY DMZ Reverse Tunnel on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Pre Reverse DMZ Jump, in order, with the same source IP followed by at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Post DMZ Jump in order from the same source IP from the previous sequence, within 1 minutes
RED_list: Event description: The system has detected events flowing from DMZ addresses and opening a new connection to a remote network.  This could be considered suspicious activity.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should not contribute to the naming of the associated offense(s)
